queue.title: Expose solver strategy selection honestly
queue.prompt: After the explicit two-phase baseline and solver quality reporting exist, expose solver selection through the WASM and web boundaries without changing the default product behavior. Keep the bounded solver as the default, allow explicit two-phase baseline selection when available, surface configured limits, solver mode, metrics, replay-backed playback, invalid-input, not-found-within-limits, and unsupported or unavailable strategy outcomes. Label limited solver paths honestly and do not duplicate cube logic or claim optimality or a 20-move guarantee.
acceptance: WASM solve accepts a documented solver strategy or returns JavaScript-friendly unsupported or unavailable strategy errors while preserving existing default behavior
frontend boundary and UI represent solver mode, configured limits, success metrics, invalid input, not-found-within-limits, and unsupported or unavailable strategy outcomes distinctly
web UI keeps the bounded solver as the default and labels the two-phase path honestly as limited or experimental while full generated tables are absent
successful solutions from any exposed strategy are replay-verified by the Rust engine before success and playback continues to use engine-produced states
the deterministic solver benchmark command remains available after boundary changes and still reports the exposed engine strategies
Playwright covers the default product flow plus solver strategy selection or its honest unsupported, unavailable, or limit outcome
frontend code does not duplicate cube validation, move application, pruning lookup, or solver logic
UI text avoids unsupported optimality or 20-move guarantee claims
no large generated pruning tables, datasets, benchmark reports, or model artifacts are committed
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

queue.title: Define dataset schema and tiny generator fixture
queue.prompt: After solver quality reporting and web strategy selection exist, define a versioned dataset schema and deterministic tiny dataset generator for later ML work. Generate states through cube-engine, serialize them with an engine-owned cubie or facelet format, validate every state before writing, include explicit label semantics, deduplicate states, define deterministic train/validation/test split rules, and keep large generated data out of git. Do not integrate ML or change product solver defaults.
acceptance: dataset records include schema version, serialized engine-owned state, generation seed or source, validation metadata, and split name
labels distinguish generated scramble_depth from verified_solution_length and avoid calling generated depth optimal unless proven
states are produced, serialized, deserialized where applicable, and validated by cube-engine before writing
deduplication and train/validation/test split rules are deterministic and covered by tests
npm run dataset:test or an equivalent documented command validates the schema and tiny fixture generator
a tiny fixture dataset is commit-safe while large generated datasets, reports, and temporary artifacts are ignored or not produced
web, WASM, and E2E product behavior remains green
no ML training, learned heuristics, model checkpoints, or unsupported optimality and 20-move guarantee claims are added
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

history.title: Build deterministic cubie engine foundation
history.prompt: Implement the pure Rust cube engine foundation with cubie state, moves, notation, scrambles, validation, solved-state detection, and deterministic tests.
acceptance: cubie representation tracks corner and edge permutation and orientation as the engine source of truth
moves, inverse moves, algorithms, notation, and scrambles are parsed or applied in Rust
cube validation rejects duplicate, missing, orientation, permutation, and parity errors with structured variants
solved-state detection is available and covered by deterministic tests

history.title: Convert user facelets into validated cubies
history.prompt: Implement user-facing 54-character Kociemba facelet input, facelet-to-cubie conversion, cubie-to-facelet rendering, and structured validation errors without replacing the engine cubie representation.
acceptance: facelet parsing validates length, symbols, counts, and fixed centers
corner and edge facelets decode into CubieState and validate cubie invariants before solving
CubieState can render back to facelets for round-trip and playback support
syntax, center, decoding, and cubie-validation failures remain distinct

history.title: Expose bounded verified solver APIs
history.prompt: Expose a correct bounded solver API for cubie and facelet states with explicit limits, metrics, structured errors, and replay verification before returning success.
acceptance: IDA* and search budgets support max depth and optional max node limits
public solver types expose configuration, results, metrics, and distinct error cases
cubie and facelet solve entry points use the same Rust solver path
every successful solution is replay-verified before being returned
insufficient limits return honest not-found-within-limits failures with metrics

history.title: Expose validation, solve, and playback through WASM
history.prompt: Expose cube-engine validation, solve, solved-facelet retrieval, playback, and final solved verification to JavaScript through wasm-bindgen with JavaScript-friendly results.
acceptance: crates/wasm exists as a wasm-bindgen crate delegating to cube-engine
validation, solve, solved-facelet, and playback exports return JavaScript-friendly success and failure data
validation errors, notation errors, and not-found-within-limits results are distinct
WASM wrapper tests cover solved, shallow, invalid-input, limit, and playback paths

history.title: Build the browser solve and playback flow
history.prompt: Build a Vite React/TypeScript web app that loads the generated WASM package through a boundary, accepts facelet input, validates states, solves with visible limits, displays notation and metrics, and supports engine-backed playback.
acceptance: apps/web is buildable with React, TypeScript, and Vite
generated WASM package loading is isolated in a frontend boundary module
UI accepts facelet input, validates through Rust/WASM, and shows useful errors
solve controls expose max_depth and max_nodes before solving
successful solves display notation, solution length, metrics, and engine-backed playback states
layout has responsive desktop and mobile CSS

history.title: Protect the web product flow with Playwright
history.prompt: Configure Playwright product-flow tests for solved input, shallow valid input with replay verification, invalid input errors, and ignored E2E artifacts.
acceptance: Playwright is configured for the web app
E2E tests cover solved facelets, shallow scrambled facelets, invalid facelets, and playback reaching solved
test scripts build WASM and the web app before E2E execution
Playwright reports and test artifacts are ignored by git

history.title: Complete checked two-phase coordinate helpers
history.prompt: Add checked coordinate helpers for two-phase solver foundations, including orientation, UD-slice edge combination, corner permutation, slice-edge permutation, and U/D-edge permutation coordinates with deterministic tests.
acceptance: coordinate ranges are explicit and exported for two-phase foundations
rank and unrank helpers round-trip solved and moved states where applicable
public helper errors distinguish out-of-range indexes and invalid input permutations or orientations without panics
tests cover solved states, shallow moved states, highest valid coordinates, and invalid inputs

history.title: Define pruning-table boundary and tiny fixture
history.prompt: After the checked two-phase coordinate set is complete, define the internal pruning-table boundary and add a deliberately tiny deterministic fixture for tests. Add compact table metadata, checked lookup behavior, a safe storage or gitignore policy for future generated artifacts, and a small generator or fixture path that proves solved and shallow-state lookups without committing full-size tables. Keep product solver defaults unchanged.
acceptance: pruning-table metadata records coordinate names, dimensions, generation parameters, phase role, and version information
lookup APIs distinguish missing entries, out-of-range indexes, mismatched metadata, and available distances without panics
a deterministic tiny fixture or generator path covers solved-state and shallow-state distances
tests cover successful lookups plus corrupted, mismatched, missing, and out-of-range fixture failures
large generated pruning-table locations are documented or gitignored
the product solver does not depend on generated pruning tables by default
existing validation, solve, WASM, web, and E2E product flows remain green
no large generated pruning tables, datasets, benchmark reports, or model artifacts are committed
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

history.title: Integrate an explicit two-phase solver baseline
history.prompt: Integrate the completed coordinate helpers and pruning-table lookup boundary into a deterministic cube-engine two-phase baseline behind explicit SolverConfig selection. The baseline may remain limited while only tiny committed fixtures exist, but it must return replay-verified moves, preserve distinct invalid-input and not-found-within-limits errors, and keep the existing bounded solver as the default product path. Do not claim optimality or a 20-move guarantee.
acceptance: two-phase search can be selected explicitly through cube-engine SolverConfig without changing the default bounded solver path
solved and known shallow cubie and facelet states return replay-verified solutions through the selected two-phase path
selected two-phase behavior is documented or encoded as a limited baseline while full generated tables are absent
invalid input and not-found-within-limits errors remain distinct and include configured limits plus explored nodes where applicable
default validation, solve, WASM, web, and E2E behavior remains unchanged and green
public API names, errors, and tests avoid unsupported optimality or 20-move guarantees
no large generated pruning tables, datasets, benchmark reports, or model artifacts are committed
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

history.title: Add deterministic solver quality reporting
history.prompt: After an explicit two-phase baseline exists, add deterministic solver quality fixtures and a lightweight benchmark or report. Compare the default bounded solver and selected two-phase baseline on solved states, shallow scrambles, and selected harder known valid states. Record solver selection, configured limits, solution length, explored nodes, elapsed time, replay verification, and limit outcome. Keep results honest and reproducible without promising optimality or a 20-move guarantee.
acceptance: quality fixtures include solved, shallow, and selected harder valid facelet and cubie states generated or validated by cube-engine

reporting captures solver selection, including the default bounded solver and explicit two-phase baseline, configured limits, solution length, explored nodes, elapsed time, success, and not-found-within-limits outcomes
every reported success is replay-verified by the solver path before being included in the report
npm run solver:bench or an equivalent documented command runs the lightweight deterministic report
benchmark output is deterministic enough for regression comparison or clearly documented as local measurement output
large benchmark artifacts are gitignored or not produced
web and E2E product behavior remains green
reports avoid unsupported optimality and 20-move guarantee claims
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

blocked.title: Define dataset schema and deterministic generator
blocked.prompt: After the web product flow is covered by E2E and the classical solver baseline has quality reporting, define a versioned dataset schema and deterministic small dataset generator. Generate states through cube-engine, validate them before writing, include explicit label semantics, deduplicate states, define train/validation/test split rules, and keep large generated data out of git.
acceptance: dataset records include schema version, serialized state, generation seed or source, and validation metadata
labels distinguish generated scramble_depth from verified_solution_length and avoid calling generated depth optimal unless proven
states are produced and validated by cube-engine before writing
deduplication and train/validation/test split rules are deterministic
a tiny fixture dataset is commit-safe while large datasets are ignored
blocked.blockedReason: Requires the queued classical solver baseline and solver-quality reporting to exist before dataset labels and split semantics are meaningful.

blocked.title: Train a small cubie-derived value baseline
blocked.prompt: After dataset generation exists, implement the first ML baseline as a small Python/PyTorch MLP value network that consumes the versioned dataset fixture and predicts distance or distance bucket from cubie-derived features. Keep training and evaluation reproducible on a tiny fixture and do not integrate the model into the product solver yet.
acceptance: ML code consumes the dataset schema instead of parsing frontend color arrays as the source of truth
input features are derived from cubie representation or documented serialized engine state
training and evaluation run reproducibly on a tiny fixture
outputs are reported as value or bucket estimates without replacing validation, solving, or replay verification
no large datasets, reports, or checkpoints are committed
blocked.blockedReason: Requires a versioned generated dataset and measured classical solver baseline before ML experiments can be evaluated honestly.

blocked.title: Try learned move ordering behind explicit configuration
blocked.prompt: Add the first hybrid-search experiment by using learned scores only for move ordering, behind explicit configuration and with deterministic classical fallback. Preserve validation, limit reporting, and replay verification. Compare explored nodes, time, and solution length against the classical baseline before considering any learned pruning.
acceptance: hybrid mode is opt-in and the classical solver remains the default fallback
learned scores affect move ordering only and are not used as an admissible pruning bound
returned solutions are replay-verified before success
invalid input and not-found-within-limits errors remain distinct
benchmarks compare explored nodes, elapsed time, and solution length against the classical baseline
documentation or result text states limitations and avoids optimality or 20-move guarantees
WASM, web, and E2E product behavior remain green with hybrid mode opt-in
blocked.blockedReason: Requires dataset generation, ML baseline evaluation, and classical solver benchmarks before learned move ordering can be compared safely.

blocked.title: Evaluate advanced solver research ideas
blocked.prompt: Explore advanced search ideas such as multi-threaded search, beam search, MCTS, neural-guided search, human-like solving, or symmetry reduction only after the product flow, classical baseline, datasets, and hybrid safety rules are established. Each experiment must stay isolated with a baseline, metric, fallback, and replay verification.
acceptance: each experiment has a named baseline and measured metric
fallback deterministic solving remains available
solutions are replay-verified before success
scope remains isolated from product defaults unless later evidence justifies integration
no large generated artifacts are committed
blocked.blockedReason: Research extensions must wait until the web product flow, classical solver quality baseline, dataset generation, ML baseline, and hybrid safety path are complete.
